mechanism_narrative: Sustained low unemployment indicates a strong labor market, supporting consumer confidence and spending, which translates to positive corporate earnings and thus positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:low unemployment generally correlates with economic growth
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative or flat during periods of UNRATE below 5% for more than 60% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate drops below a certain threshold, it signals an accommodative monetary policy, reducing borrowing costs for companies and stimulating investment, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:lower interest rates typically boost equity valuations
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS crosses below 1.0% over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong retail sales and increasing housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and investment in real estate, signaling broad economic strength that drives corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.5% AND HOUST 3-month average growth > 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:consumer spending and housing are key economic drivers
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative or flat when both RSAFS and HOUST show sustained growth for more than 60% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high industrial production growth, the economy is operating near full capacity, which can lead to inflationary pressures. If CPIAUCSL also rises, it signals potential overheating, prompting central bank tightening and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 6-month annualized growth > 3% AND CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth > 3%
evidence_refs: fact:high inflation often leads to monetary tightening
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where both INDPRO and CPIAUCSL growth rates exceed 3% over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in GDP per capita indicates a healthy and expanding economy, which provides a strong fundamental backdrop for corporate earnings and thus supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP growth is a primary indicator of economic health
regime_hint: any regime with positive GDP growth
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative or flat during periods of positive GDPC1 growth for more than 60% of observations over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals a weakening labor market, indicating potential recessionary pressures and reduced consumer spending, which leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:job losses are a strong indicator of economic downturns
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where PAYEMS declines by more than 50,000 over a 3-month average over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is low and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is also low, it creates a 'goldilocks' scenario of stable prices and accommodative monetary conditions, which is highly supportive of equity valuations and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:low inflation and low rates are favorable for equities
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances where both CPIAUCSL growth is below 2.5% and FEDFUNDS is below 2.0% over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a contraction in manufacturing and overall supply-side activity, indicating a weakening economy that will negatively impact corporate earnings and thus S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:industrial production declines often precede recessions
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where INDPRO shows a 1-month decline greater than 0.5% over any 3-year period.
